 {"id":"bd-k4lj.3","title":"Capability Override Injection","description":"Runtime capability injection for testing.

## Requirements
- Override any capability flag
- Test-only API (compile-time gated)
- Thread-local overrides
- Stack of overrides for nesting

## Implementation
- CapabilityOverride in ftui-core
- with_capability_override() scope
- Override precedence rules

## Tests
- Override application
- Nested scope handling
- Cleanup on scope exit

## Acceptance Criteria
- Deliver the behavior implied by the title: Capability Override Injection
- Alien Artifact: define invariants + failure modes; if heuristics exist, specify an explainable decision rule (evidence ledger / Bayes factors where applicable)
- Extreme Optimization: baseline -> profile -> opportunity matrix; for each perf change include an isomorphism proof + golden output checksums
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures; add property tests for core invariants where applicable
- E2E: scripts with verbose JSONL logging (env, capabilities, timings, seed, checksums) + deterministic mode

 {"id":"bd-k4lj.4","title":"Terminal Quirk Simulation","description":"Simulate terminal-specific quirks and bugs.

## Requirements
- tmux nested cursor quirk
- Screen line wrap behavior
- Windows console limitations
- Documented quirk catalog

## Implementation
- Quirk enum with behaviors
- QuirkSet per profile
- Quirk application in VirtualTerminal

## Tests
- Each quirk reproduction
- Quirk interaction
- Documentation accuracy

## Acceptance Criteria
- Deliver the behavior implied by the title: Terminal Quirk Simulation
- Alien Artifact: define invariants + failure modes; if heuristics exist, specify an explainable decision rule (evidence ledger / Bayes factors where applicable)
- Extreme Optimization: baseline -> profile -> opportunity matrix; for each perf change include an isomorphism proof + golden output checksums
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures; add property tests for core invariants where applicable
- E2E: scripts with verbose JSONL logging (env, capabilities, timings, seed, checksums) + deterministic mode

 {"id":"bd-k4lj.5","title":"Simulator Integration with Harness","description":"Integrate simulator with test harness.

## Requirements
- Run snapshot tests against profiles
- Cross-profile comparison mode
- CI integration guide
- Profile selection via env var

## Implementation
- FTUI_TEST_PROFILE env var
- profile_matrix test helper
- CI workflow for multi-profile

## Success Criteria
- Full test suite runs against dumb terminal
- Profile differences documented
- CI catches capability regressions

## Acceptance Criteria
- Deliver the behavior implied by the title: Simulator Integration with Harness
- Alien Artifact: define invariants + failure modes; if heuristics exist, specify an explainable decision rule (evidence ledger / Bayes factors where applicable)
- Extreme Optimization: baseline -> profile -> opportunity matrix; for each perf change include an isomorphism proof + golden output checksums
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures; add property tests for core invariants where applicable
- E2E: scripts with verbose JSONL logging (env, capabilities, timings, seed, checksums) + deterministic mode

-{"id":"bd-k4lj.6","title":"Capability Simulator E2E Test Suite","description":"## Purpose
End-to-end validation of terminal capability simulation for testing.

## Test Scenarios

### 1. Profile Accuracy (cap_profile_e2e.rs)
- xterm-256color capabilities
- xterm-16 color limitations
- vt100 basic cursor only
- dumb terminal minimal features

### 2. Capability Override (cap_override_e2e.rs)
- Runtime capability injection
- Thread-local isolation
- Override stacking
- Cleanup on scope exit

### 3. Degradation (cap_degrade_e2e.rs)
- Color fallback behavior
- Style attribute fallback
- Cursor movement fallback
- Mouse support fallback

### 4. Quirk Simulation (cap_quirk_e2e.rs)
- tmux nested cursor quirk
- Screen line wrap behavior
- Windows console limitations
- iTerm2 specific features

### 5. Integration (cap_integration_e2e.rs)
- Full app under different profiles
- Profile switching mid-test
- Cross-profile snapshots
- Performance comparison

## Logging Requirements
- INFO: Profile switches
- DEBUG: Capability queries and results
- TRACE: Quirk applications
- Metric: Render output differences

## Script Location
scripts/e2e/capability_sim/

## CI Matrix
- Run full suite under each profile
- Compare output across profiles
- Verify no capability leaks

## Acceptance Criteria
- Deliver the behavior implied by the title: Capability Simulator E2E Test Suite
- Alien Artifact: define invariants + failure modes; if heuristics exist, specify an explainable decision rule (evidence ledger / Bayes factors where applicable)
- Extreme Optimization: baseline -> profile -> opportunity matrix; for each perf change include an isomorphism proof + golden output checksums
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures; add property tests for core invariants where applicable
- E2E: scripts with verbose JSONL logging (env, capabilities, timings, seed, checksums) + deterministic mode

 {"id":"bd-kkn1","title":"Implement Cross-Terminal Clipboard tests","description":"# Implement Cross-Terminal Clipboard tests

## Background
Part of Cross-Terminal Clipboard Abstraction (bd-2d2v). Tests ensure clipboard works across terminals.

## Unit Tests
- osc52_encoding_correct: OSC 52 sequence properly base64 encoded
- fallback_to_system_clipboard: When OSC 52 fails, use pbcopy/xclip/wl-copy
- read_clipboard_works: Can read back what was written
- large_content_handled: Multi-KB content works correctly
- binary_content_base64_encoded: Non-text content encoded properly

## Integration Tests
- copy_paste_roundtrip_in_iterm: Full cycle works in iTerm2
- copy_paste_roundtrip_in_wezterm: Full cycle works in WezTerm
- copy_paste_roundtrip_in_kitty: Full cycle works in Kitty
- fallback_works_on_dumb_terminal: System clipboard used when OSC 52 unavailable

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All unit tests pass
- [ ] Integration tests pass on major terminal emulators
- [ ] Graceful fallback when clipboard not available","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:33:38.691212422Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T20:46:03.313064402Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T20:46:03.312998518Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-kkn1","depends_on_id":"bd-2d2v","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:18Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-l0ko","title":"Add tracing spans to all render phases","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:24:10.149920273Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T20:34:12.480899941Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T20:34:12.480879152Z","close_reason":"Added tracing spans to all render phases: render_frame (info), present_ui (info), present_inline/altscreen diff_compute+emit+clear_ui+scroll_region (debug), emit_diff (debug with run count)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-l0ko","depends_on_id":"bd-em01","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:18Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-l8x9","title":"Visual FX Modularization: Composable Backdrops + Library Effects (Metaballs/Plasma) + Optional GPU","description":"Extract the demo-only visual effects (metaballs/plasma first) into `ftui-extras` as reusable, composable, high-performance backdrops and effect primitives; enable alpha-correct compositing behind real widgets; design an optional GPU backend with silent failover to CPU.

## Context
The showcase implements impressive FX directly inside:
- `crates/ftui-demo-showcase/src/screens/visual_effects.rs`

Today those algorithms are demo-owned and mostly render via:
- `ftui_extras::canvas::{Painter, Canvas}`

That prevents reuse behind real widgets (markdown panels, dashboards) where we want:
- correct alpha compositing into `cell.bg`
- legibility/contrast knobs (scrim)
- deterministic, allocation-free rendering

## Success Criteria (User-Visible)
- `ftui-extras` exposes a feature-gated `visual_fx` module:
  - core types (`BackdropFx`, `FxQuality`, `ThemeInputs`)
  - `Backdrop` widget + composition helpers
  - extracted `MetaballsFx` + `PlasmaFx` (with presets + quality scaling)
- Backdrops can be composed behind real widgets with predictable results:
  - base fill (opaque) + effect opacity + optional scrim
  - markdown-over-backdrop demo integration (bd-l8x9.7)
- Demo showcase uses the extracted library code:
  - Phase 1: remove per-frame Painter allocations (bd-l8x9.5.1)
  - Phase 2: metaballs/plasma via BackdropFx (cell-space) (bd-l8x9.5.2)
  - Optional: regain hi-res via canvas adapters (bd-l8x9.6 + bd-l8x9.5.3)
- Tests are first-class:
  - unit tests for bg-only invariant, alpha correctness, determinism, tiny areas (bd-l8x9.8)
  - E2E scripts with detailed, actionable logs (bd-l8x9.8.1/8.2)
- Performance is defended:
  - benchmarks / budgets for backdrop+FX (bd-l8x9.9)
- Optional GPU acceleration (if enabled) is seamless:
  - uses GPU when available
  - **never** errors or panics; always silently falls back to CPU (bd-l8x9.11–14)

## Non-Goals (This Epic)
- Extracting every single showcase effect immediately.
- Forcing GPU requirements; CPU is the baseline.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:30:26.788253805Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T19:27:00.821685369Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T19:27:00.821667114Z","close_reason":"All 14 subtasks completed: API spec, backdrop widget, metaballs/plasma extraction, demo integration, canvas adapters, UI integration, blending tests, performance profiling, docs, GPU backend (spike, impl, fallback, heuristics)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["architecture","extras","performance","visual"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-l8x9","depends_on_id":"bd-3vbf","type":"related","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:18Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":499,"issue_id":"bd-l8x9","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Context

The demo showcase has genuinely impressive full-screen visual effects (metaballs, plasma, fire, matrix, etc.) implemented directly inside:
- `crates/ftui-demo-showcase/src/screens/visual_effects.rs`

Right now these effects are *demo-owned* algorithms. They render via:
- `ftui_extras::canvas::{Painter, Canvas}` (Braille/sub-cell rasterization)

This is great for a wow-factor demo, but it prevents reuse inside real UI components (markdown panels, dashboards, forms) where we want:
- alpha-correct compositing behind widgets
- predictable contrast/legibility guarantees
- zero-flicker and deterministic presentation
- insanely high performance (no per-frame allocations, cache-friendly loops)

## Goal

Turn “demo visual effects” into **library-grade, trivially composable primitives** in `ftui-extras`:
- Effects can render as a **cell-background backdrop** behind arbitrary widgets (markdown, panels, lists).
- Effects can optionally also render as **high-resolution canvas output** for full-screen screensaver-like demos.
- Composition must be correct: use alpha blending and preserve scrollback/inline mode invariants.

Initial extraction scope:
- Metaballs + Plasma (because they are visually strong and structurally simple).

## Why This Serves FrankenTUI’s Core Goals

FrankenTUI’s differentiators are correctness + determinism + composability. Background effects are a perfect stress-test:
- If the API makes backdrops easy to layer correctly, *everything* becomes easier to compose.
- If the rendering model enforces zero surprise (no terminal corruption; no implicit I/O), it aligns with the one-writer rule.

We already have key low-level primitives:
- `PackedRgba::over()` implements Porter-Duff SourceOver.
- `Buffer::push_opacity()` + background compositing in `Buffer::set()` enable layered blending.

## Architectural Direction (High-Level)

We introduce a “backdrop render target” alongside the existing canvas target:

1) Backdrop target (cell-space):
- effect computes a `w*h` array of `PackedRgba` backgrounds
- widget writes those colors into `frame.buffer` as background-only pixels

2) Canvas target (sub-cell space):
- effect computes sub-pixel intensity/color into a `Painter` (Braille/blocks)
- `Canvas` renders it as glyphs

The critical composability primitive is the **Backdrop widget** (background-only), because it composes under any widget without affecting glyphs.

## Proposed Public API Sketch (Subject To Iteration)

```rust
pub enum FxQuality {
    Full,
    Reduced,
    Minimal,
}

pub trait BackdropFx {
    /// Fill `out` (len = w*h) with background colors in cell-space.
    ///
    /// Design constraints:
    /// - No per-call allocations (caller owns buffers).
    /// - Deterministic output given inputs.
    /// - Must tolerate tiny areas.
    fn render_bg(
        &mut self,
        out: &mut [PackedRgba],
        w: u16,
        h: u16,
        t: f32,
        theme: &ThemeInputs,
        quality: FxQuality,
    );
}

pub struct Backdrop<E> { /* effect + opacity + base fill + scrim policy */ }

impl<E: BackdropFx> Widget for Backdrop<E> { /* writes only cell.bg */ }
```

Theme input is an explicit decision point (see tasks):
- likely accept either `ftui_style::ResolvedTheme` (preferred stable surface)
- or a small `ThemeInputs` struct derived from `ftui-extras::theme::ThemePalette`

## Legibility / Contrast Policy

Backdrops must be safe behind text.
We achieve this by default through:
- an explicit opaque base fill (theme bg)
- effect composited with configurable opacity (usually 0.15–0.40)
- optional scrim overlay (theme overlay) under text-heavy regions

This creates a reliable invariant: text widgets can remain “transparent bg” and still be readable.

## Performance Non-Negotiables

- Zero allocations on the render hot path.
- Cache-friendly scanning (row-major), minimize branching.
- Avoid per-frame `Painter::for_area()` allocations in showcase; reuse buffers.
- Map `Frame.buffer.degradation` -> `FxQuality` so heavy effects degrade gracefully.

## Optional GPU Backend (Feature-Gated)

If a GPU is available, it should be used *only* as an internal accelerator and must:
- never panic or error to the user
- silently fall back to CPU forever if init fails
- be deterministic enough for interactive use (snapshot tests should default to CPU)

Planned implementation shape:
- `fx-gpu` feature using `wgpu`
- `FxBackend::Auto` with cached “GPU available” decision via `OnceLock`
- GPU computes the same `PackedRgba` cell-space buffer; terminal emission remains CPU

## Deliverables

- `ftui-extras`: new visual-fx/backdrop module(s) with stable-ish API
- extracted `MetaballsFx` + `PlasmaFx` using the new API
- demo showcase refactor to use the extracted library code
- tests for blending correctness + determinism, and perf sanity checks

## Non-Goals (For This Epic)

- extracting every single demo effect immediately (metaballs/plasma first)
- requiring GPU (GPU is optional and must never break CPU-only builds)
- changing terminal output/presenter mechanics
","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:30:57Z"}]}
